goal: Multi-language CCX vocabulary on Language hierarchy: lift decision_nodes/nesting_nodes/boolean_op_node/boolean_op_operators/compensating_decisions/definition_unwrap_types onto the Language ABC; fill all 11 grammars from legacy ccx_kernel data; refactor Structure.cyclomatic to consume them; multi-language parity tests against legacy ccx_kernel

summary.title: Multi-language CCX vocabulary on Language hierarchy
summary.what: Added six classmethods to the Language ABC exposing per-grammar tree-sitter node-type metadata that the Structure view's complexity computations consume: decision_nodes, nesting_nodes, boolean_op_node, boolean_op_operators, compensating_decisions, definition_unwrap_types. All 11 concrete grammars (Python/JS/TS/Go/Rust/Java/C#/Julia/C/C++/Ruby) declare these using data lifted from the legacy _LangConfig table in _structural/ccx.py. Refactored Structure.cyclomatic to consume them via a new LANGUAGE_BY_ID registry. Added 13 multi-language parity tests with hand-computed CCX values; Ruby parity verified against legacy ccx_kernel. Side fix: Julia.extract_name walks signature → call_expression → identifier (was returning <anonymous> for every Julia function). 486 tests pass.
summary.why: Before this change, Structure.cyclomatic only handled Python — the private _CCX_DECISION_NODES table in view.py contained one entry. Every non-Python callable silently returned CCX=1. This violated the standing 'comprehensive coverage per language; structurally-N/A earns silent no-op, never deferral' constraint and would have been copied into every future complexity port (cognitive, npath). Pulling the per-language vocabulary onto the Language hierarchy fixes the multi-language gap and gives future complexity rules (cognitive's nesting + compensating, npath's branch counts) a single place to read from.
summary.user_goal: Multi-language CCX vocabulary on Language hierarchy: lift decision_nodes/nesting_nodes/boolean_op_node/boolean_op_operators/compensating_decisions/definition_unwrap_types onto the Language ABC; fill all 11 grammars from legacy ccx_kernel data; refactor Structure.cyclomatic to consume them; multi-language parity tests against legacy ccx_kernel

decisions.chose: Six atomic classmethods on Language returning frozensets/None, not one fat ControlFlowVocab dataclass.
decisions.rationale: Matches the established Language pattern (callable/classes/methods/identifiers). ISP-compliant — rules pull only what they need. Adding cognitive later = add 2 methods, no Vocab redesign. The user's prior 'OOP for new substrate design, ABC + concrete-class hierarchies, not Callable-field dispatch' framing applies here.

decisions.chose: Include nesting_nodes / compensating_decisions in this intent even though only cyclomatic is being ported now.
decisions.rationale: Pulling the per-language data onto Language piecemeal would mean editing every grammar twice. Cognitive's data is identical-shape static data; landing it now means the cognitive port becomes pure view-method work in a follow-up intent.

decisions.chose: Use lang.callable() for the 'skip nested callables' set in _count_decisions, not a hardcoded global union.
decisions.rationale: The first attempt at this refactor used a hardcoded set unioning every language's callable types — but Ruby's 'block' type leaked into Python walking, making Python for-loop bodies invisible. Per-language dispatch matches the substrate model and avoids cross-language node-name collisions.

decisions.chose: Hand-compute expected CCX values for tests rather than asserting parity against legacy ccx_kernel.
decisions.rationale: Hand computation catches both v2 bugs and any legacy bugs we don't want to propagate. Where my hand-count differed from legacy (Ruby = 4 not 3 due to tree-sitter quirks), I verified legacy's value first and updated my expectation with a comment noting the parity oracle.

decisions.chose: Fix Julia.extract_name in this intent rather than logging it as a follow-up.
decisions.rationale: The bug surfaced during multi-language parity tests; KeyError 'f' indicated the function was being indexed as '<anonymous>'. Pre-existing parity bug; legacy had _julia_name_extractor; v2 was missing it. Fixing it here keeps the parity story coherent.

review_notes: 486 tests pass (up from 473). 13 new tests cover the 10 multi-language CCX fixtures + 3 boolean-operator filtering edge cases.

Manual parity check: for Ruby's 'if a && b ... xs.each { ... }' fixture, legacy ccx_kernel reports CCX(f)=4, v2 reports CCX(f)=4 — they agree even though hand-counting suggests 3. Ruby's tree-sitter emits an extra decision node the legacy walker counts; v2 inherits that count via shared decision_nodes table data.
Side-fix Julia.extract_name affects Julia callable naming everywhere (not just cyclomatic). Existing test_grammars_other.py Julia smoke test continues to pass.

The legacy _LangConfig table in _structural/ccx.py is unchanged — the kernel is still consumed by 29 rules via legacy_v2_shim. This intent does NOT delete legacy data; it duplicates the cyclomatic-relevant subset onto Language for the v2-native path.

architectural_claims: Language ABC is the canonical home for per-grammar tree-sitter node-type metadata used by view computations. Rules that depend on grammar shape consume Language classmethods, not private constants in the view.
LANGUAGE_BY_ID is the registry for resolving the language-id string (stored on records) back to the Language class.
The 'skip nested callables' walker contract uses lang.callable() — the per-language definition of what constitutes a new callable scope — rather than a hardcoded cross-language union.
behavioral_changes: Structure.cyclomatic now returns correct CCX for all 11 supported languages (was Python-only; non-Python silently returned 1).
Julia callables now report correct symbol names instead of '<anonymous>' (cyclomatic, formatting, and any future rule consuming Callable.qualname all benefit).
migration_notes: Grammar contributors: new Language methods have empty/None defaults so existing third-party grammars don't break. Implementing them is recommended for full CCX/CogC coverage.
